settings.compare_intro: A wet cloth holds moisture against seeds, breeding mould and that musty smell. The mesh-lid jar drains every rinse completely — clean airflow, faster sprouts, no surprises.
settings.box_note: Just add water and a kitchen counter. The kit handles the rest.

settings.heading: Beginner Friendly

settings.heading: No Smell, No Mess
settings.description: Self-draining mesh lid keeps sprouts clean. No daily cloth changes.

settings.heading: WhatsApp Help

settings.heading: Seed Refills

settings.title: SOAK THE SEEDS
settings.description: Add 2 tbsp seeds to the jar. Cover with water. Soak 6–8 hours overnight.

settings.title: DRAIN + RINSE
settings.description: Tilt jar to drain through the sprouting lid. Rinse twice. Lay jar at angle on the stand.

settings.title: RINSE TWICE DAILY
settings.description: Morning + evening. Drain fully each time. Keep at room temp, no direct sunlight.

settings.title: HARVEST IN 2–3 DAYS
settings.description: Sprouts are ready when tails are 1–2 cm long. Refrigerate. Eat over a week.

settings.right_s: Mesh drains fully, airflow stays clean

settings.left_s: Drips on the counter, soaks the surface
settings.right_s: Self-draining jar sits on the stand
Proper rinsing and drainage prevents it
Glass jar + steel mesh last for years

settings.heading: 10× MORE ENZYMES

settings.heading: FRESH IN 2–3 DAYS
settings.description: From dry seed to crunchy sprout in under 72 hours. No soil, no sun, no waiting weeks for a plant.

settings.heading: PROTEIN-DENSE

settings.question: How is this different from sprouting in cloth?
settings.answer: Cloth holds moisture against the seeds, which causes mould and that musty smell. The mesh lid drains water completely after every rinse — seeds get airflow, no standing water. Cleaner, faster, no smell.

settings.question: Do I need sunlight?
settings.answer: No. Sprouts grow best at room temperature, away from direct sunlight. A kitchen counter works perfectly.

settings.question: Which seeds can I sprout in this jar?
settings.answer: Moong, chickpea, alfalfa, fenugreek (methi), mustard, broccoli, radish, lentils. Avoid kidney beans (toxic when raw-sprouted) and any seed not labeled sprouting-grade.

settings.question: How often do I rinse?
settings.answer: Twice a day — morning and evening. Drain fully each time. The whole process takes under 30 seconds per rinse.

settings.question: How long do harvested sprouts last?
settings.answer: Refrigerated, 5–7 days. Rinse before storing and pat dry. Eat raw in salads, stir-fry briefly, or add to khichdi at the end.

settings.question: Is the jar dishwasher-safe?
settings.answer: Yes — borosilicate glass and stainless steel mesh both go in the dishwasher. Hand-wash also works.

settings.question: Can kids do this?
settings.answer: Yes — it's a great kitchen science project. The transformation from seed to sprout in 2–3 days is genuinely fun to watch.

settings.question: What if my seeds don't sprout?
settings.answer: Most often the seeds were too old or stored in heat. We replace any non-germinating seed pack within 30 days — just write to support@gutbasket.com with a photo.
